regression_ml.summary: This is a classical machine learning regression problem. Success depends heavily on feature quality and domain expertise.
regression_ml.ScaleRecommendation: Scikit-learn is suitable for prototyping at any scale. XGBoost/LightGBM are recommended for production tabular data regardless of size due to superior performance. Use Spark MLlib only when data exceeds single-machine memory (typically 50+ GB) or requires distributed processing.

classification_ml.summary: This is a classical machine learning classification problem. Focus on class balance and appropriate evaluation metrics.
classification_ml.ScaleRecommendation: Logistic Regression for baseline and interpretable models. Gradient Boosting (XGBoost/LightGBM) is the industry standard for tabular data at any scale and typically outperforms deep learning on structured data. Consider Deep Learning only for unstructured inputs (images, text) or extremely complex feature interactions.

nlp_general.summary: NLP complexity varies dramatically. Start simple and escalate only when necessary.
nlp_general.ApproachGuidance: Simple tasks (sentiment, topic classification): Use embedding APIs (OpenAI, Cohere, Sentence Transformers) + classical ML. Medium complexity (NER, summarization): Fine-tune smaller transformer models (e.g., DeBERTa, DistilBERT) or use LLM APIs with structured prompting. Complex tasks (domain-specific understanding): Fine-tune larger models with domain data. Document processing: Consider specialized models like LayoutLM or DocTR.

computer_vision_general.summary: Computer Vision requires significant computational resources and labeled data. Transfer learning is almost always the right approach.
computer_vision_general.ApproachGuidance: Always start with transfer learning from pre-trained models (EfficientNet, ConvNeXt, YOLO, DINOv2). Training from scratch is rarely justified; only consider it with >50k labeled images and truly unique visual domains not represented in existing models. Data augmentation is essential regardless of dataset size.

ocr_services.summary: OCR is a solved problem for most use cases. Focus on post-processing and quality validation rather than building custom models.
ocr_services.Approach: Use managed OCR services (Google Vision AI, Azure Read API, AWS Textract) unless you have highly specialized documents that require custom models.

rag_search.summary: Information retrieval spans from simple search to complex RAG systems. Complexity depends on semantic understanding requirements.
rag_search.ApproachGuidance: Simple keyword search: Elasticsearch/OpenSearch. Semantic search: Embedding models + vector DB (Qdrant, Chroma, Weaviate, Pinecone, Milvus). Question answering: RAG pipeline combining retrieval with LLM generation. Consider hybrid search (keyword + semantic) for best results. Chunking strategy and embedding model selection are critical to RAG quality.

generative_ai.summary: Content generation is powerful but requires careful output validation and bias monitoring.
generative_ai.ApproachGuidance: Start with prompt engineering on existing LLM APIs (GPT-4, Claude, Gemini). For domain-specific knowledge, use RAG before considering fine-tuning. Fine-tuning is expensive ($10k-$100k+), time-consuming, and only justified for consistent style, format, or highly specialized domain output. Always implement output guardrails and human review for production use.

decision_support.summary: Decision support ranges from simple rule engines to complex ML models. Always implement human oversight for critical decisions.
decision_support.ApproachGuidance: Start with rule-based systems for well-defined decisions. Use ML when patterns are complex or rules are hard to define explicitly.

anomaly_detection.summary: For unlabeled data, this approach identifies data points that deviate from the norm. Success depends on clear separation between normal and abnormal patterns.
anomaly_detection.ApproachGuidance: Use algorithms like Isolation Forest, Local Outlier Factor (LOF), or Autoencoders. Threshold tuning is critical to balance sensitivity and false positives.

anomaly_detection_supervised.summary: With fully labeled data, anomaly detection becomes a classification problem. This is a more robust approach if labels are reliable.
anomaly_detection_supervised.ApproachGuidance: Treat anomalies as a class. Use classifiers like Random Forest or Gradient Boosting. Address significant class imbalance with techniques like SMOTE or class weights.

anomaly_detection_semisupervised.summary: With partially labeled data, use a semi-supervised approach. Use the labeled examples to guide the model's learning process.
anomaly_detection_semisupervised.ApproachGuidance: Use techniques like label propagation or build a model on the labeled portion and use it to score unlabeled data. This can be more accurate than purely unsupervised methods.

optimization.summary: Optimization problems require clear objective functions and constraints. Most business optimization can be solved with classical methods.
optimization.ApproachGuidance: Use classical optimization (linear/nonlinear programming) for well-defined problems. Consider RL for complex, dynamic environments.

data_engineering.summary: Data processing is infrastructure-heavy. Focus on scalability, reliability, and monitoring from the start.
data_engineering.ScaleGuidance: Pandas for datasets up to ~1GB (loads everything into memory, typically needs 2-5x the data size in RAM). Polars/DuckDB for 1-50GB (multi-threaded, memory-efficient, handles out-of-core processing). Spark only when data exceeds single-machine capacity (typically 50GB+) or requires distributed cluster processing.

agentic_ai.summary: CAUTION: Agentic AI is a rapidly maturing but high-risk technology. Many enterprises have deployed narrow agentic systems in production, but scaling remains challenging. Robust guardrails, observability, and human-in-the-loop oversight are essential.
agentic_ai.RiskLevel: HIGH RISK - Maturing Technology with Governance Challenges

data_processing_structured.DataProcessing: Standard SQL and pandas/polars workflows apply.

data_processing_text.DataProcessing: Text preprocessing and NLP pipelines required.

data_processing_images.DataProcessing: Image preprocessing and augmentation pipelines required.

data_processing_audio.SpecializedTools: Audio processing libraries (librosa, speechrecognition) or API services.

approach_small_data.RecommendedApproach: Transfer learning, pre-trained models, data augmentation, synthetic data generation, or simple classical ML. Quality of data matters more than quantity at this scale.

tools_medium_data.RecommendedTools: Pandas, Scikit-learn, XGBoost suitable for this scale.

processing_large_data.DataProcessing: Spark, Polars, Dask, or chunked processing recommended.

processing_massive_data.Infrastructure: Cloud data platforms (Databricks, Snowflake, BigQuery) recommended.

processing_streaming_data.DataProcessing: Stream processing platforms (Kafka, Flink, Kinesis) required.

arch_realtime.Architecture: Streaming architecture with online learning capabilities.

arch_daily_batch.Architecture: Batch processing with daily model retraining pipeline.

xai_critical.ModelChoice: Prefer interpretable models (Linear/Logistic Regression, Decision Trees, Rule-based systems).

xai_important.XAI: Basic explainability tools for model debugging and validation.

multilingual_support.ModelType: Multi-lingual models (XLM-R, mBERT) or language-specific models.
multilingual_support.DataRequirements: Balanced datasets across all target languages.

mlops_automated_retraining.MLOps: Automated retraining pipelines (Kubeflow, Vertex AI Pipelines, MLflow) required.
mlops_automated_retraining.Monitoring: Comprehensive model and data drift detection.

tuning_high_recall.ModelTuning: Optimize for High Recall. Accept higher false alarm rate to minimize missed cases.

tuning_high_precision.ModelTuning: Optimize for High Precision. Model must be very confident before making positive predictions.

tuning_balanced.ModelTuning: Optimize for balanced F1-score or overall accuracy.

compliance_strict.Explainability: Likely mandatory for audit and compliance.
compliance_strict.Auditing: Complete audit trail of all model decisions and data processing.

xai_mandatory.Explainability: MANDATORY: Use SHAP or LIME. Strongly prefer interpretable models (Linear/Logistic Regression, Decision Trees).
xai_mandatory.ModelComplexity: Avoid deep learning unless interpretability can be maintained.

hitl_mandatory.Strategy: MANDATORY Human-in-the-Loop (HITL) validation before any automated action. System should augment, not replace, human experts.
hitl_mandatory.Validation: Formal verification methods and extensive testing required.
